author: mark
text:
SKILL.md: cover reviewer use-cases

The skill was written exclusively from a worker POV. "Never run
bl close" is misleading because the same agent that reads this
skill is sometimes a reviewer and needs to know how to approve
and reject.

- Add a Roles section up top distinguishing worker vs reviewer.
- Split the workflow into Worker Workflow and Reviewer Workflow.
- Reviewer section: bl list --status review, bl show, git show
  delivered_in, bl close from repo root (with the explicit
  "cannot close from within the worktree" error reference), and
  bl update status=in_progress for rejection.
- Reframe the absolute "Never run bl close" as worker-specific
  "Don't close your own task".
- Add the close-from-worktree case to Error Recovery.

Doc-only change. include_str! pulls SKILL.md into the bl binary,
so make check (tests + clippy + line length + 100% coverage) ran
green against the new text.

author: mark
text: Reviewer feedback: the rule is really about NOT closing while standing inside the worktree, not about whose work it is. Solo-agent flow (claim, work, review, cd back to main, close) is fine. Reframe accordingly.
